$comment: Frozen allowlists for the Prisma schema naming-convention linter (manifest/scripts/lint-schema.mjs). Both lists capture the EXISTING physical reality so the linter exits 0 on today's schema while blocking NEW drift. Do NOT add entries to make a new model pass — fix the model instead (PascalCase name + @@map("snake_case")). Removing an entry that is still in the schema will make the linter re-flag it; only remove an entry after the underlying model is renamed/remapped via a migration. See docs/database/README.md § Schema Naming Conventions for the rules and the rationale.

rules.R1: A Prisma model name must be PascalCase, unless listed in legacySnakeCaseModels.
rules.R2: A model's resolved physical table name (the @@map value if present, else the model name verbatim) must be snake_case, unless the model is listed in pascalCaseTableExceptions.
rules.R3: Allowlist hygiene: every entry below must still correspond to a model in schema.prisma; stale entries are flagged so the allowlist does not rot.

legacySnakeCaseModels.reason: Pre-Manifest legacy models whose Prisma model name is itself snake_case (model name == physical table, no @map on fields). Frozen: do not add new snake_case-named models — new models use PascalCase + @@map. These 31 predate the PascalCase+@@map convention and renaming them is a data-migration cost not worth paying.

pascalCaseTableExceptions.reason: Models whose physical table is PascalCase, not snake_case. Two sub-groups: (a) 4 models with an explicit @@map("PascalCase") locked in by historical migrations (Tenant, ActivityFeed, EmployeeDeduction, OutboxEvent); (b) 16 PascalCase models with NO @@map, added in Task 0.3 from IR entities, whose table defaults to the verbatim PascalCase model name. Frozen: new PascalCase models MUST add @@map("snake_case"). Renaming these tables requires a migration and is deferred.
